document.heading: Private Detention Centers - Citywide Ban
An Ordinance Concerning Private Detention Centers - Citywide Ban

para.heading: Application of section.
para.text: This section and its listing of certain prohibited uses:
is not exhaustive; and
may not be construed to imply that any use not listed here is a permitted or conditional use.

para.heading: Listing.
para.text: The following uses are prohibited in all zoning districts of the City:
crude oil terminals;
incinerators;
junk or scrap storage and yards;
nuclear power plants;
private detention centers;
solid waste sanitary landfills;

storage on barges and belt conveyor systems used for the transfer of materials, but this prohibition does not apply to the continuous process of unloading or loading processed metal (as defined in § 1-308) for and during its transfer to or from a docked barge or vessel awaiting shipment; and
vehicle dismantling facilities.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Government facility" means a structure or land that is operated by a government agency.

para.heading: Inclusions.
para.text: "Government facility" includes agency offices, storage yards, public works facilities, recreation: indoor, and utility facilities that are operated by a government agency.

para.heading: Exclusion.
para.text: "Government facility" does not include a private detention center.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Greenhouse" means a structure that is:
devoted to the protection or cultivation of flowers or other tender plants; and
constructed chiefly of glass, glass-like or translucent material, cloth, or lath.

para.heading: By any other name.
para.text: "Greenhouse" includes a "high tunnel", "hoop-house", "cold-frame", or similar structure.

para.heading: Gross floor area.
para.text: See "Floor area: Gross".

para.heading: Gym.
para.text: See "Health and fitness center".

para.heading: Ham radio equipment.
para.text: See "Amateur (ham) radio equipment".

para.heading: Health-care clinic.
para.text: "Health-care clinic" means a facility for the examination and treatment of individuals on an outpatient basis by 1 or more physicians, dentists, chiropractors, physical therapists, or other licensed healthcare practitioners.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Health and fitness center" means a gym or other facility that:
is designed for physical fitness or weight reduction; and
contains equipment, such as weight resistance machines, treadmills, stationary bicycles, whirlpools, saunas, showers, and lockers, for that purpose.

para.heading: Inclusions.
para.text: "Health and fitness center" includes the following accessory uses designed and intended primarily for patrons of the facility:
retail sales; and
a restaurant or refreshment stands.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Heavy sales, rental, or service" means a sales, rental, or service establishment that maintains a service or storage area that is outdoors or in a structure that is only partially enclosed.

para.heading: Inclusions.
para.text: "Heavy sales, rental, or service" includes:
large-scale home improvement centers;
industrial supply stores;
lumberyards;
heavy equipment parts, sales and rental; and
playground equipment, sales and rental.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Heliport" means a designated landing area for discharging or picking up passengers or goods by helicopter or similar vertical-lift aircraft.

para.heading: Inclusions.
para.text: "Heliport" includes terminal facilities for passengers, goods, aircraft servicing, or storage.

para.heading: Helistop.
para.text: "Helistop" means an area of land, water, or structure that is used or intended to be used for the landing and take-off of helicopters or similar vertical-lift aircraft, but without facilities for servicing or basing these aircraft.

para.heading: Holiday sales lot.
para.text: See "Seasonal or holiday sales lot".

para.heading: Home occupation.
para.text: "Home occupation" means an occupation that:
is carried on in a dwelling unit by a resident of the dwelling unit; and
is secondary to the use of the dwelling unit for residential purposes.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Homeless shelter" means a facility that provides temporary housing to homeless or transient individuals.

para.heading: Inclusions.
para.text: "Homeless shelter" includes accessory services such as counseling or vocational training.

para.heading: Horse stable.
para.text: "Horse stable" means any structure or land within the City at or from which horses are boarded, bred, sold, or rented for riding, driving, or other purposes.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Hospital" means an institution that provides health services, primarily inpatient medical or surgical care for the sick or injured.

para.heading: Inclusions.
para.text: "Hospital" includes related facilities integral to the hospital, such as laboratories, outpatient centers, health-care clinics, helistops, training facilities, classrooms, staff offices, on-site medical waste and storage facilities, and central service facilities.

para.heading: "Guest unit" defined.
para.text: In this section, "guest unit" means any room or group of rooms that forms a single habitable unit occupied or designed or intended to be occupied for sleeping or living purposes.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Hotel" or "motel" means a building that:
is not a dwelling;

contains 3 or more guest units occupied or designed or intended to be occupied by guests who, even though they might share common areas and facilities, do not form a single housekeeping unit and do not provide compensation under a single lease for occupancy of the facility; and
offers lodging in these guest units to members of the general public.

para.heading: Exclusions.
para.text: "Hotel" or "motel" does not include a bed and breakfast or a rooming house.

para.heading: Housing Commissioner; Commissioner of Housing.
para.text: "Housing Commissioner" or "Commissioner of Housing" means the Commissioner of Housing and Community Development or the Commissioner's designee.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Impervious surface" means any surface that does not allow stormwater to infiltrate into the ground.

para.heading: Inclusions.
para.text: "Impervious surface" includes rooftops, driveways, sidewalks, and pavement.

para.heading: Exclusions.
para.text: "Impervious surface" does not include ballasted railroad tracks.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Incinerator" means a combustion unit that uses controlled flame combustion for the thermal destruction of solid waste, including municipal waste, industrial waste, hazardous waste, special medical waste, or sewage sludge.

para.heading: Inclusions.
para.text: "Incinerator" includes any:
infrared incinerator; or
plasma arc incinerator.

para.heading: Indoor entertainment.
para.text: See "Entertainment: Indoor".

para.heading: Indoor recreation.
para.text: See "Recreation: Indoor".

para.heading: Industrial arts studio.
para.text: See "Arts studio: Industrial".

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Industrial boat repair facility" means a facility with 5 or more slips (wet or dry) used solely for the manufacture, assembly, or repair of commercial or recreational watercraft.

para.heading: Exclusions.
para.text: "Industrial boat repair facility" does not include docking, storage, or sales of recreational watercraft.

para.heading: Parapet.
para.text: "Parapet" means the extension of a false front or wall above a roof-line.

para.heading: Parking.
para.text: "Parking" means the parking, storage, housing, or keeping of a motor vehicle, whether self-service or valet-service, long-term or short-term, ticketed or metered, for special events only, or otherwise.

para.heading: Parking garage (principal use).
para.text: "Parking garage (principal use)" means a structure the principal use of which is to provide off-street parking for motor vehicles, whether for compensation or not.

para.heading: Parking garage (residential, detached).
para.text: "Parking garage (residential, detached)" means a structure, adjacent to but detached from a dwelling, for the off-street parking of motor vehicles, with no commercial operations.

para.heading: Parking lot.
para.text: "Parking lot" means an open area the principal use of which is to provide off-street parking for operable motor vehicles, whether for compensation or not.

para.heading: Parking: Off-street.
para.text: "Parking: Off-street" means parking spaces for motor vehicles on premises other than a street or other right-of way.

para.heading: Parking: On-street.
para.text: "Parking: On-street" means parking spaces for motor vehicles located on a street or other right-of-way.

para.heading: Parking space.
para.text: "Parking space" means a designated area, whether open or enclosed, that is used for the parking of a motor vehicle.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Park" or "playground" means a non-commercial facility that serves the reflective and recreational needs of residents and visitors.

para.heading: Inclusions.
para.text: "Park" or "playground" includes:
ball fields and basketball courts;
exercise stations;
skateboarding areas;
pet areas;
hiking and biking trails;
stream and lake areas;
forest and meadowland areas;
reflective, historical, cultural, and educational areas; and
structures that have indoor recreation facilities.

para.heading: Party wall.
para.text: "Party wall" means a wall that:
extends from the foundation continuously through all stories to or above the roof;
separates 1 building from another; and
is in joint use by each building.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Passenger terminal" means a facility or location the principal use of which is handling, receiving, and transferring passenger traffic.

para.heading: Inclusions.
para.text: "Passenger terminal" includes terminals for aircraft, train, bus, and watercraft passengers.

para.heading: Pawn shop.
para.text: "Pawn shop" means a business that:
lends money on the deposit or pledge of personal property or other items of value, other than securities or printed evidences of indebtedness; or
purchases personal property or other items of value, subject to the seller's option to buy them back at a stipulated price.

para.heading: Permitted use.
para.text: See "Use: Permitted"

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Person" has the meaning stated in § 1-107(a) {"Person: In general"} of the City Code's General Provisions Article.

para.heading: Qualified inclusion of governmental entities.
para.text: Notwithstanding § 1-107(b) {"Person: Exclusion"} of the General Provisions Article, in this subtitle "person"also includes, except as used in Title 19, Subtitle 2 {"Enforcement"} of this Code for the imposition of civil or criminal penalties, a governmental entity or an instrumentality or unit of a governmental entity.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Personal services establishment" means an establishment that provides recurrent needed services of a personal nature.

para.heading: Illustrations.
para.text: Typical examples of a "personal services establishment" include:
beauty shops, including micro-blading services, and barbershops;
tanning salons;
animal grooming establishments;
electronics repair shops;
laundromats, dry cleaners, and tailors; and
certified massage therapists or similar licensed professionals.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Place of worship" means a building, together with its accessory buildings and uses, where individuals regularly assemble for religious purposes and related social events.

para.heading: Inclusions.
para.text: "Place of worship" includes:
group housing for individuals under religious vows or in religious orders; and
accessory programs that provide care for children or for elderly or functionally impaired adults.

para.heading: Planning Director; Director of Planning.
para.text: "Planning Director" or "Director of Planning" means the Director of the Department of Planning, as appointed under City Charter Article VII, § 73, or the Director's designee.

para.heading: Playground.
para.text: See "Park; Playground".

para.heading: Porch.
para.text: "Porch" means a structure that projects from the exterior wall of a building, has direct access to the street level of the building, and may be covered by a roof or eaves.

para.heading: Principal building.
para.text: See "Building: Principal".

para.heading: Principal use.
para.text: See "Use: Principal".

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Printing establishment" means a facility for the reproduction of written or graphic materials on a custom-order basis for individuals or businesses.

para.heading: Illustrations.
para.text: Typical examples of a "printing establishment" include:
photocopying;
blueprinting;
offset printing; and
facsimile sending and receiving.

para.heading: In general.
para.text: "Private detention center" means any building, facility, or structure that:
is owned or operated by a private, nongovernmental entity pursuant to an agreement with a federal, state, or local government entity; and
is used, in whole or in part, to house or detain an individual, at the direction of a government agency:
prior to trial sentencing;
during the term of the individual's sentence;
for a federal immigration violation; or
for another judicial or administrative process or proceeding.

para.heading: Exclusions.
para.text: "Private detention center" does not include a residential-care facility.

para.heading: Private pier.
para.text: "Private pier" means facilities with 4 or fewer slips, used exclusively for private, recreational purposes by the owner and the owner's guests.

para.heading: Processed metal.
para.text: See "Junk or scrap storage and yards".
